name: Pikipek

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon feeds on berries, whose leftover seeds become the ammunition for the attacks it fires off from its mouth.

name: Tornadus

attacks.name: Energy Wheel
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

name: Night Pokémon Center
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, remove all damage counters from that Pokémon. If both are tails, discard all Energy cards attached to that Pokémon.

name: Double Gust
rules: If you have any Benched Pokémon, your opponent chooses 1 of them and switches it with your Active Pokémon. Then, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rush
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads. Hitmonchan can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Smash Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Bursting Balloon
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Poochyena
flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Zen Blade
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zen Blade during your next turn.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Rayquaza

attacks.name: Turbo Storm
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

name: Potion Energy
rules: If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any. Potion Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Vileplume ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Block Dust
abilities.text: As long as Vileplume ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards (except for Supporter cards) from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Special Formula
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Palette of Friends
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

name: Sabrina's ESP
rules: Attach Sabrina's ESP to 1 of your Pokémon with Sabrina in its name. At the end of your turn, discard Sabrina's ESP. If that Pokémon uses and attack that involves flipping coins, Sabrina's ESP lets you re-flip those coins once. If you do, re-flip all the coins.

name: Delcatty

attacks.name: Replace
attacks.text: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

attacks.name: Spearhead
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.text: Farfetch'd does 10 damage to itself.

name: Diggersby

attacks.name: Mountaintop Mining
attacks.text: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: As powerful as an excavator, its ears can reduce dense bedrock to rubble. When it's finished digging, it lounges lazily.

name: Leftovers
rules: Attach Leftovers to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
If the Pokémon Leftovers is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Breaker Bazooka
attacks.text: Discard all Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Hitmonchan ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

name: Holon Energy WP
rules: Holon Energy WP provides Colorless Energy.

If the Pokémon that Holon Energy WP is attached to also has a basic Water Energy card attached to it, prevent all effects, excluding damage, done to that Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy WP is attached to also has a basic Psychic Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 0. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy WP is attached to Pokémon-ex.

name: Electrode ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Energy Bomb
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard Electrode ex and all the cards attached to it (this counts as Knocking Out Electrode ex). If you do, search your discard pile for 5 Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) in any way you like. This power can't be used if Electrode ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Crush and Burn
attacks.text: You may discard as many Energy as you like attached to your Pokémon in play. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy you discarded.

name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Bite Off
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Wild Barking
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Inviting Trap
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It makes a conical pit in desert sand and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.

name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness.

name: Moonlight Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
The Retreat Cost for each Psychic and Darkness Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is 0.

name: Aurorus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Frozen Charm
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it can't be Paralyzed. (If any of those Pokémon are Paralyzed, remove that Special Condition.)

attacks.name: Crystal Breath
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

name: Pidgeot-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Feather Lance
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Blaine's Quiz #2
rules: Put a card from your hand face down in front of you. Your opponent guesses whether it is an Energy card, a Trainer card, or a Pokémon (Basic or Evolution) card. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 2 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 2 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

name: Delinquent
rules: Discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, your opponent discards 3 cards from his or her hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Staggering Steps
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, this Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The chances of two Spinda having identical spot patterns is less than one in four billion.

name: Professor Sycamore
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, switch Hitmontop with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Scizor ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Danger Perception
abilities.text: As long as Scizor ex's remaining HP is 60 or less, Scizor ex does 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Scizor ex by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Sandaconda

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

name: Regigigas LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Regigigas. Regigigas LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Sacrifice
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and that Pokémon is Knocked Out. Then, search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, attach them to Regigigas, and remove 8 damage counters from Regigigas. This power can't be used if Regigigas is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Giga Blaster
attacks.text: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck. Then, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it. Regigigas can't use Giga Blaster during your next turn.

name: M Steelix-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Canyon Axe
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Dunsparce

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It transforms the kindness and joy of others into happiness, which it stores in its shell.

name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can play this card only when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Intensifying Burn
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Vaporeon ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

abilities.name: Blue Ray
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Vaporeon Star from your hand onto your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions and 3 damage counters from each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Bedrock Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Burrows at high speed in search of food. The tunnels it leaves are used as homes by Diglett.

name: Mega Catcher
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Mega Evolution Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Salamence

abilities.name: Dragon Wind
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It flies around on its wings, which have grown in at last. In its happiness, it gushes hot flames, burning up the fields it passes over.

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Miracle Harmony
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Sing attack. This attack does 10 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feels of those who hear it.

name: Magearna-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mystic Heart
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Metal Energy attached to it. (Existing effects are not removed.)

attacks.name: Soul Blaster
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Soul Blaster attack's base damage is 60.

name: Togetic

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.text: Your opponent draws a card.

flavorText: To share its happiness, it flies around the world seeking kind-hearted people.

name: Gengar ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Trainer card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Prize Count
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Gible

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Charmander.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Suicune

abilities.name: Extreme Speed
abilities.text: Suicune's Retreat Cost is Colorless less for each Water Energy attached to Suicune.

attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon races across the land. It is said that north winds will somehow blow whenever it appears.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Bibarel

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Tumbling Tackle
attacks.text: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by the people nearby.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At nights, its kind is drawn by light.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

name: Moo-Moo Milk
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. Remove 2 damage counters times the number of heads from that Pokémon. If the Pokémon has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Snorlax LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Snorlax. Snorlax LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Big Appetite
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Snorlax is your Active Pokémon, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. If you do, Snorlax is now Asleep. This power can't be used if Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Exercise
attacks.text: You may discard as many Energy cards as you like from your hand. If you do, remove that many damage counters from Snorlax.

name: Muk

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: Because they scatter germs everywhere, they've long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.

name: Life Herb
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your Pokémon, and remove all Special Conditions and 6 damage counters from that Pokémon (all if there are less than 6).

name: Trumbeak

attacks.name: Bullet Seed
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: By bending its beak, it can produce a variety of call and brand itself a noisy nuisance for its neighbors.

name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Ferocious Bellow
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

name: Pokémon Trader
rules: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: M Pidgeot-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Cyclone
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Secret Mission
rules: Look at your opponent's hand. Then, you may discard as many other cards as you want from your hand and draw that many cards.

name: PlusPower
rules: Attach PlusPower to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your turn.
If the Pokémon PlusPower is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Maintenance
rules: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can't shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can't play this card.) Then, draw a card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Taillow
flavorText: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

name: Steelix ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Poison Resistance
abilities.text: Steelix ex can't be Poisoned.

attacks.name: Metal Charge
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on Steelix ex.

attacks.name: Mudslide
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fighting Energy attached to Steelix ex and choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Wigglytuff ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Robo Substitute Team Flare Gear
rules: Play this card as if it were a 30 HP Colorless Basic Pokémon. At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat. If this card is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize Cards for it.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Weak Kneed
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

name: Mudkip ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spring Back
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Mawile-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wonder Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Latias & Latios-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Buster Purge
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aero Unit-GX
attacks.text: Attach 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to it during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Coalossal

abilities.name: Tar Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Fire Energy card, a Fighting Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

abilities.name: Lightweight
abilities.text: You pay Colorless less to retreat Hoppip for each Grass Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Careless Tackle
attacks.text: Hoppip does 10 damage to itself.

name: Flygon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dusty Defense
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Desert Hurricane
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge-GX
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Flabébé
flavorText: It's OK. No one will take away your flower. You look cute even when you're angry.

name: M Rayquaza-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Break
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Noibat

attacks.name: Destructive Sound
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard all Item cards you find there.

flavorText: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Ground Stream
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gigaton Shake
attacks.text: During your next turn, your Pokémon can't attack. (This includes Pokémon that come into play on that turn.)

flavorText: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

name: Latias ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.name: Dreamy Mist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Basic Benched Dragon Pokémon.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

name: Patrat

attacks.name: Glance
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

name: Thundurus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raiden Knuckle
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunderous Noise
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Defender
rules: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

name: Spritzee

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

name: Delinquent

name: Blaine's Quiz #3
rules: Put a Basic or Evolution card from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent the name of an attack of that card. Your opponent guesses the name of that card. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 3 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 3 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

name: Porygon-Z

flavorText: In order to create a more advanced Pokémon, an additional program was installed, but apparently it contained a defect that makes it move oddly.

name: Gardevoir-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Luminous Blade
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Tickling Machine
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent sets aside all the cards in his or her hand face down. Nobody may look at those cards. At the end of your opponent's next turn, your opponent puts those cards back into his or her hand. If tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Watchog

attacks.name: Scrutinize
attacks.text: Look at the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck, discard 1 of them, and put the other card back.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It knows how people and Pokémon feel by looking at their auras. It doesn't approach dangerous opponents.

name: Mountain Ring
rules: Prevent all damage done to Benched Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Anorith
flavorText: When restored Anorith are released into the ocean, they don't thrive, because the water composition has changed since their era.

name: Shining Kabutops
rules: You can't have more than 1 Shining Kabutops in your deck.

attacks.name: Lightning Cut
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage and does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If tails, this attack does 30 damage and Shining Kabutops does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Water Slash
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Shining Kabutops but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Don't apply Resistance.

flavorText: This ancient Pokémon uses its razor-sharp claws to cut open its prey and gain access to its blood.

name: Full Heal
rules: Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

name: Cresselia LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Cresselia. Cresselia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Full Moon Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from either player's Pokémon to another Pokémon (yours or your opponent's). This power can't be used if Cresselia is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Moon Skip
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Me First
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

name: Tyranitar ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

name: Goldeen

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Goldeen.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Vaporeon ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, your opponent draws up to 4 cards.

attacks.name: Fastwave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Bouffalant

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 40 more damage to your opponent's Grass Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Wooper

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Bone Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: M Gardevoir-EX

attacks.text: Discard as many of your Benched Pokémon as you like. This attack does 10 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

name: Furfrou

abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Cutoff
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: No two Spinda have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

name: Sail Fossil
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Amaura you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Old Amber Aerodactyl
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Aerodactyl you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: See Through

flavorText: It has a perfect sense of time. Whatever happens, it keeps rhythm by precisely tilting its head in time.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This amiable Pokémon is easy to train. But when battle is joined, it shows its ferocious side.

abilities.name: Unburden
abilities.text: If Drifloon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, Drifloon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless more.

attacks.name: Big Explosion

name: Umbreon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Darker Ring
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Umbreon ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Black Cry
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat or use any Poké-Powers during your opponent's next turn.

name: Miasma Valley
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Whenever any player puts a Basic Pokémon (excluding Grass or Psychic Pokémon) from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

name: Slurpuff

attacks.name: Lap Up
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

flavorText: The cakes Slurpuff makes always look just like that one Pokémon it likes.

name: Shining Mewtwo
rules: You can't have more than 1 Shining Mewtwo in your deck.

attacks.name: Reflect Shield
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to Shining Mewtwo during your opponent's next turn (even if Shining Mewtwo is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shining Mewtwo from that attack (any other effects of attacks still happen) and do 20 damage to the attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psyburst
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Shining Mewtwo or this attack does nothing. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses its highly developed psychic powers to defeat its enemies before they even have time to think.

name: Rayquaza-GX

abilities.name: Stormy Winds
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If you do, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of basic Grass and basic Lightning Energy attached to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tempest-GX
attacks.text: Discard your hand and draw 10 cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads

attacks.text: Don't apply Resistance.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Snivy

attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy from your hand to this Pokémon.

rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Bebe's Search
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can't play this card.)

name: Aerodactyl-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.text: Put up to 3 damage counters on Duskull. Then, put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to the Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.

name: Surskit

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch Horsea with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Pokémon Ranger
rules: Remove all effects of attacks on each player and his or her Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Sylveon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

rules: Attach Koga's Ninja Trick to your Active Pokémon with Koga in its name. If this Pokémon goes to your Bench, discard this card. When your opponent attacks, you may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon (before damage or other effects of attacks).

name: Challenge!
rules: Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can't do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.

name: Purugly

attacks.name: Own the Place
attacks.text: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Toss Aside
attacks.text: Discard random cards from your opponent's hand until they have 3 cards in their hand.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

attacks.name: Sudden Swipe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Energy cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Night Raid
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a Pokémon from it.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

name: Entei-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.text: Attach a Fire Energy from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Darkrai. Darkrai LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.text: Each basic Darkness Energy card attached to your Darkness Pokémon now has the effect "If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance)." You can't use more than 1 Dark Shadow Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Endless Darkness
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

name: Cassius
rules: Shuffle 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Wally
rules: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-EX) and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. You can use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Brock's Grit
rules: Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Vermilion City Gym
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Whenever a player attacks with a Pokémon with Lt. Surge in its name, he or she may flip a coin. If heads, and if that Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, the attacking Pokémon does 10 damage to itself in addition to whatever its attack usually does.

name: Shauna
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Loop-the-Loop
attacks.text: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Its favorite foods are Wingull and Pikipek. It attacks with a powerful kick and grasps them firmly in its talons.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon.

name: Armaldo

attacks.name: Ancient Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Unidentified Fossil card in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It lived on land and went out into the sea to hunt for prey. Its sharp claws were its greatest weapon.

name: Axew
flavorText: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

name: Darkness Energy
rules:
If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon. At the end of every turn, put 1 damage counter on the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to, unless it's Darkness or has Dark in its name.
Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Clobbopus
flavorText: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

name: Gyarados-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stormy Seas
attacks.text: Flips a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Beast Ring
rules: You can play this card only if your opponent has exactly 3 or 4 Prize cards remaining.
Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Ultra Beasts. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Stonjourner

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

name: Buneary
flavorText: By extending its rolled up ears and striking the ground, it can bound so high it surprises itself.

name: Pokédex HANDY910is
rules: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Dredge Up
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Sandile's still not good at hunting, so it mostly eats things that have collapsed in the desert. It's called "the cleaner of the desert."

name: Yungoos
flavorText: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

name: Servine

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When it gets dirty, its leaves can't be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

name: Pokémon Fan Club
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Counter Energy
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.

If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, and if this card is attached to a Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.

name: PlusPower
rules: Attach PlusPower to your Active Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard PlusPower. If this Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Shining Noctowl

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play (including Sneasel ex). This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Flight
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly.

name: Mr. Mime ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magic Evens
abilities.text: If Mr. Mime ex would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Mr. Mime ex if that damage is 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140, 160, or 180.

name: Salamence ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Salamence ex's type is Fire until the end of your turn.

attacks.text: You may do up to 40 damage instead of 80 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
