name: Puzzle of Time
rules:
You may play 2 Puzzle of Time cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
• If you played 2 cards, put 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Max Potion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Kartana-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Slice Off
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gale Blade
attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Blade-GX
attacks.text: Take a Prize card. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Darkrai

attacks.name: Abyssal Sleep
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Grandiose Fangs
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon during your next turn (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Alola, where it's warm all year round, is a comfortable environment for this Pokémon. Sometimes you'll even see it outside of deserts.

name: Manectric-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Simultaneous Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Doduo Delivery
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Fossil Researcher
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Cracking Voice
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

name: M Gardevoir-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brilliant Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Fairy Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Meloetta-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brilliant Voice
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

name: Ultra Ball
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards, you can't play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ultra Ball
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards, you can't play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Guzma
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Fervor
rules: Show the top 3 cards of your deck to all players. Put any Fire Energy cards there into your hand and discard the rest.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Spoil the Fun
attacks.text: If you go second, this attack does 60 more damage during your first turn.

flavorText: When its delicate pride is wounded, or when the gold coin on its forehead is dirtied, it flies into a hysterical rage.

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Incessant Peck
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It stands up to massive opponents, not out of courage, but out of recklessness. But that is how it gets stronger.

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of either player's Evolved Pokémon, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon, and put it into that player's hand.

name: Quick Ball
rules: Reveal cards from your deck until you reveal a Pokémon. Show that Pokémon to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other revealed cards back into your deck. (If you don't reveal a Pokémon, shuffle all the revealed cards back into your deck.)

name: Nickit

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: Blend Energy WaterLightningFightingMetal
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy. When this card is attached to a Pokémon, this card provides Water, Lightning, Fighting, or Metal Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for any Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Pangoro
flavorText: This rowdy Pokémon boasts great physical strength. Many Trainers are also smitten by its lively character.

name: Reverse Valley
rules: Choose which way this card faces before you play it. Any damage done to this ↓ player's Metal Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Choose which way this card faces before you play it. The attacks of this ↓ player's Darkness Pokémon do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Riolu
flavorText: It knows how people and Pokémon feel by looking at their auras. It doesn't approach dangerous opponents.

name: Skull Fossil
rules: Play Skull Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Skull Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Skull Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Skull Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Skull Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Skull Stone
abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, if Skull Fossil would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Empoleon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Empoleon. Empoleon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Supreme Command
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your opponent's hand without looking and put them face down next to the Defending Pokémon. (These cards are not in play or in your opponent's hand.) At the end of your opponent's next turn, return those cards to your opponent's hand. This power can't be used if Empoleon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Impact
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Empoleon can't attack during your next turn.

name: Alolan Exeggutor-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tropical Head
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dragon Hammer
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Tower-Go-Round-GX
attacks.text: Move any number of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Ancient Crystal
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Regirock, Regice, Registeel, or Regigigas this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Max Revive
rules: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile on top of your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Meowth
flavorText: When visiting a junkyard, you may catch sight of it having an intense fight with Murkrow over shiny objects.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't Darkness. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Metang
flavorText: It flies at high speeds around the skies. When it finds its prey, Metang takes a firm grip with its sharp claws and never lets go.

name: Lycanroc

attacks.name: Dangerous Rogue
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness.

name: Galarian Obstagoon

abilities.name: Untamed Shout
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Pachi
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Staryu and remove 3 damage counters from Staryu.

flavorText: Its body is virtually composed of water. It shoots strange beams from its crystal-like eyes.

name: Fresh Water Set
rules: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Brooklet Hill
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Water Pokémon or Basic Fighting Pokémon and, put it onto their Bench, and shuffle their deck.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Lady
rules: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: No matter where in the world it goes, it knows where its nest is, so it never gets separated from its Trainer.

name: Malamar V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brain Shake
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Gardevoir Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gardevoir-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.

name: Crabrawler
flavorText: Its hard pincers are well suited to both offense and defense. Fights between two Crabrawler are like boxing matches.

name: Exp. Share
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Sniff Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It loves eating mushrooms that grow under dead grass. It also finds hot springs while foraging.

name: Muscle Band
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Gengar-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Night Attack
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Corridor
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Shaymin

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude and the message it delivers.

name: Lysandre ◇
rules: For each of your Fire Pokémon in play, put a card from your opponent's discard pile in the Lost Zone.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

name: Ho-Oh-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rebirth
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may flip a coin. If heads, put this Pokémon onto your Bench and attach 3 different types of basic Energy cards from the discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Rainbow Energy
rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Make 'Em Pay
attacks.text: If your opponent has 4 or more cards in their hand, they reveal their hand. Discard cards you find there until your opponent has exactly 4 cards in their hand.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Although the jewel on its forehead appears to be a different color than those of Alolan Persian, it's mostly made of the same material.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.

name: Braviary

abilities.name: Big Wing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wild Edge
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Doduo Delivery
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Wing Flick
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Males swing the plumage on their heads to threaten others, but females are better at flying.

name: Choice Band
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon-GX or Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Transparent Walls
rules: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from attacks done to your Benched Pokémon. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Silvally-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gyro Unit
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Turbo Drive
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rebel-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Levitation Field
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Leg Quake
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It analyzes its opponents with more accuracy than a supercomputer, which enables it to calmly back them into a corner.

name: Malamar VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Jammer
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

name: Sableye V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lode Search
attacks.text: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Crazy Claws
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Alolan Persian

attacks.name: Empty Threat
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It looks down on everyone other than itself. Its preferred tactics are sucker punches and blindside attacks.

attacks.name: Confusion Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Cynthia
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

abilities.name: Energy Barrier
abilities.text: If Mr. Mime would be damaged by an attack, reduce that damage by 10 for each basic Energy card attached to Mr. Mime. The maximum amount of damage that can be reduced by Energy Barrier is 20.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

name: Korrina
rules: Search your deck for a Fighting Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Sylveon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dress Up
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Precious Ribbon
attacks.text: Move a Fairy Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 50 damage from that Pokémon.

name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Riolu
flavorText: It knows how people and Pokémon feel by looking at their auras. It doesn't approach dangerous opponents.

name: Guzma
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Scizor Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Scizor-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: If your opponent's Bench isn't full, look at his or her hand. Choose 1 Basic Pokémon you find there and put it onto your opponent's Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Potion
rules: Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Exploud

attacks.name: Dangerous Concert
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its roar in battle shakes the ground like a tremor—or like an earthquake has struck.

name: Infernape LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Infernape. Infernape LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Burning Head
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Discard the other 2 cards. This power can't be used if Infernape is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flare Up
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for 8 Fire Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck. (This attack does nothing if you don't have 8 Fire Energy cards in your discard pile.)

name: Team Galactic's Wager
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, and you and your opponent play "Rock-Paper-Scissors." The player who wins draws up to 6 cards. The player who loses draws up to 3 cards. (You draw your cards first.)

name: Thievul

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Krokorok really hates it when its body gets cold. On nights when the temperature drops, it digs deep into the desert sands.

attacks.name: Max Voltage
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Pikachu. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Attract
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: When its opponent can't be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away.

name: Groudon Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes Primal Groudon-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Malamar-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hyper Hypnosis
abilities.text: When you attach an Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: MAXamar
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

name: Crabominable

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Freezing Punch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It stores coldness in its pincers and pummels its foes. It can even smash thick walls of ice to bits!

name: Taillow

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Taillow's Peck attack's base damage is 30.

flavorText: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

name: Rockruff
flavorText: As they develop, their disposition grows more violent and aggressive. Many Trainers find them too much to handle and abandon them.

name: Gladion
rules: Look at your face-down Prize cards and put 1 of them into your hand. Then, shuffle this Gladion into your remaining Prize cards and put them back face down. If you didn't play this Gladion from your hand, it does nothing.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp Panic
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it spots prey, even at a distance of over 30 miles, it swims through the desert as if it were water, then jumps out and chomps them.

name: Warp Point
rules: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon.

name: Rockruff

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: As they develop, their disposition grows more violent and aggressive. Many Trainers find them too much to handle and abandon them.

name: Audino

abilities.name: Busybody
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your hand, you may reveal it. If you do, heal 10 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon. Then, discard this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hip Bump
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

name: Tierno

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

The attacks of the Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
(If this card is attached to anything other than a Fighting Pokémon, discard this card.)

name: M Heracross-EX

name: Torterra LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Torterra. Torterra LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Forest Murmurs
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can't be used if Torterra is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Vigorous Dash
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Torterra does 30 damage to itself.

name: Skitty

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Umbreon

name: Armor Fossil
rules: Play Armor Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Armor Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Armor Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Armor Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Armor Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Armor Stone
abilities.text: Whenever Armor Fossil would be damaged by your opponent's attack, flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, reduce that damage by 10.

name: Impidimp
flavorText: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

name: Dodrio

abilities.name: Retreat Aid
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is ColorlessColorless less.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this Pokémon's poison seeps in.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Benched Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

name: Florges-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lead

attacks.name: Bright Garden

name: Arceus

attacks.name: Type Switch
attacks.text: Choose Grass, Fire, Water, Lightning, Psychic, Fighting, Darkness, Metal, Fairy, or Dragon type. Until the ends of your next turn, this Pokémon is that type.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

name: Choice Band
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon-GX or Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Kyogre Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes Primal Kyogre-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool card in this way, this attack does 70 more damage.

name: Jirachi

attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 7 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Lucario

(Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If 2 of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If all of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Lysandre Labs
rules: Pokémon Tool cards in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no effect.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Glaceon-EX

rules: Play Skull Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Skull Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Skull Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Skull Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Skull Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.text: During your opponent's turn, if Skull Fossil would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Kiawe
rules: Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Metal Frying Pan
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Tyranitar-GX

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Grimmsnarl

abilities.name: Dark Oath

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Lillie

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Greninja

attacks.name: Bring Down

attacks.name: Mist Slash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Honchkrow LV.X

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Water Pokémon, discard this card.)

name: Lana
rules: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Keldeo

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.text: This Pokémon's attacks do 50 more damage to your opponent's Darkness Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Sacred Sword
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword during your next turn.

flavorText: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are too fast to follow.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A Pokémon that appears to be a plant. It captures unwary prey by dousing them with a toxic powder.

name: Giratina LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Giratina. Giratina LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Invisible Tentacles
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent discards 1 card from his or her hand. (If your opponent can't discard 1 card, your opponent's Pokémon can't attack.) You can't use more than 1 Invisible Tentacles Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Darkness Lost
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If any of your opponent's Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from this attack, put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone instead of discarding it.

name: Dawn Wings Necrozma

attacks.name: Gulf Stream
attacks.text: If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 20 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sword of Dawn
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Lunala no longer has a will of its own. Now under the control of Necrozma, it continuously expels all of its energy.

name: Alakazam-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Kinesis
abilities.text: When you play M Alakazam-EX from your hand to evolve this Pokémon, before it evolves, you may put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon and 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Suppression
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Raging Herd
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Tauros and Tauros-GX.

flavorText: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Rototiller
attacks.text: Shuffle 4 cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Nest Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: High Flight
attacks.text: Each player reveals his or her hand. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Item cards revealed.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Burst
attacks.text: Discard all basic Fire Energy or all basic Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.

name: Xerosic
rules: Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) and discard it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Eternatus VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Eternal Zone
abilities.text: If all of your Pokémon in play are Darkness type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can't put non-Darkness Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)

attacks.name: Dread End
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Darkness Pokémon in play.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers' aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

name: Palkia G LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Palkia G. Palkia G LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Lost Cyclone
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Any player who has 4 or more Benched Pokémon chooses 3 of his or her Benched Pokémon. Put the other Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to them in the Lost Zone. (You choose your Pokémon first.) This power can't be used it Palkia G is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Palkia G. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Gyarados-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Draconic Disaster
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Dread Storm-GX
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Dusk Mane Necrozma

attacks.name: Dusk Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Rusty Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: This is its form with it is devouring the light of Solgaleo. It pounces on its foes and then slashes them with the claws on its four limbs and back.

name: Toxicroak

abilities.name: More Poison
abilities.text: Put 2 more damage counters on your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Spore Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Paras. Attach it to Paras. This counts as evolving Paras. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Archen

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Once thought to be the ancestor of all bird Pokémon, some of the latest research suggests that may not be the case.

name: Aerodactyl

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Fossil Fangs
attacks.text: If you don't have any Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Restored from DNA found in amber, this Pokémon exhibited ferocity that was greater than expected. Some casualties resulted.

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Mysterious Treasure
rules: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Psychic or Dragon Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It likes the shovel on its head, so Sandygast will get serious and fight any children who come to take it back.

name: M Alakazam-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zen Force
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Destructor Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Roller Skates
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Excadrill

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill's rampant digging.

name: Yanma

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch Yanma with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Yanma during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won't miss prey—even those behind it.

attacks.name: Sleep Bubble
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already have a Pokémon Tool attached to them). Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Galarian Meowth

abilities.name: Evolution Roar
abilities.text: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Galarian Perrserker, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

name: Escape Rope
rules: Each player switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. (Your opponent switches first. If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, he or she doesn't switch Pokémon.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It draws prey near with its blinking lights and then wraps them up in its long tentacles and holds them in place.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't Darkness. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Gyarados-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stormy Seas
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mount Lanakila
rules: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless more.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Acro Bike
rules: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Escape Board
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is Colorless less, and it can retreat even if it's Asleep or Paralyzed.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Alolan Diglett

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its golden hairs function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

name: Teddiursa

attacks.name: Honey Snack
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: M Manectric-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Turbo Bolt
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Poison Barb
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Teddiursa
flavorText: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Power Charger
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Naganadel-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beast Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Ultra Beasts in play.

attacks.name: Jet Needle
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Stinger-GX
attacks.text: Both players shuffle their Prize cards into their decks. Then, each player puts the top 3 cards of their deck face down as their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Mysterious Treasure
rules: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Psychic or Dragon Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Giratina-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Palossand

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Each of its grains of sand has its own will. Palossand eats small Pokémon and siphons away their vital essence while they're still alive.

name: Alolan Golem-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Super Electromagnetic Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Heavy Rock-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any cards from their hand during their next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Umbreon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Veil of Darkness
attacks.text: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.

attacks.name: Endgame
attacks.text: If your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.

name: Olivia
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon-GX, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ultra Recon Squad
rules: Discard up to 2 Ultra Beast cards from your hand. Draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Archeops

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This ancient Pokémon's plumage is delicate, so if anyone other than an experienced professional tries to restore it, they will fail.

name: Shadow Circle
rules: Each Pokémon that has any Darkness Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent's) has no Weakness.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Twiddle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Picnic Weather
attacks.text: Put a Teddiursa from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, attach an Energy card from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

name: Nihilego-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Empty Light
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Symbiont-GX
attacks.text: Add the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck to their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Meloetta

attacks.name: Soprano Wave
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Entrancing Melody
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

name: Serperior

abilities.name: Royal Heal
abilities.text: At any times between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Leaf Tornado
attacks.text: Move as many Grass Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Genesect-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Drive Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

attacks.name: Rapid Blaster
attacks.text: Discard as many Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon as you like. This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy you discarded.

name: Nickit

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: M Gengar-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Phantom Gate
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Acro Bike
rules: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Escape Board
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is Colorless less, and it can retreat even if it's Asleep or Paralyzed.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ferroseed

attacks.name: Reaction
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn't very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's impulsive, selfish, and fickle. It's very popular with some Trainers who like giving it the attention it needs.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Sandshrew
flavorText: To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

name: Repeat Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as 1 of your Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

name: Scizor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hack Off
attacks.text: Discard a Pokémon Tool and a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Shaymin LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Shaymin. Shaymin LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Thankfulness
abilities.text: Each of your Grass Pokémon (excluding any Shaymin) gets +40 HP. You can't use more than 1 Thankfulness Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Seed Flare
attacks.text: Choose as many Grass Energy cards from your hand as you like and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached in this way.

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

name: Bubble Coat
rules: Attach Bubble Coat to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
As long as Bubble Coat is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon has no Weakness. If that Pokémon is damaged by an opponent's attack, discard this card at the end of the turn.

name: Alolan Dugtrio

attacks.name: Digging Dash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

name: Ultra Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Photon Geyser
attacks.text: Discard all basic Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Sky-Scorching Light-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if the total of both players' remaining Prize cards is 6 or less. Put 6 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Galarian Perrserker

attacks.name: Claw Dagger
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

name: Hugh
rules: Each player either draws or discard cards until he or she has 5 cards in his or her hand. (Your opponent does this first.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Hypnotic Reign
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may discard a Pokémon you find there and use one of that Pokémon's non-GX attacks as this attack.

attacks.name: Dark Pressure
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: When it comes to strong hypnosis, there's an endless number of people who utilize Malamar for their nefarious deeds.

name: Zangoose

attacks.name: Head Hunt
attacks.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal any number of Pokémon you find there, and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

flavorText: Its fur would all stand on end if it smelled a Seviper nearby. Its sharp claws tear up its foes.

name: M Gyarados-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blast Geyser
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Phanpy.

name: Ether
rules: Reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If it is not a basic Energy card, return it to the top of your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Field Blower
rules: Choose up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards and Stadium cards in play (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Smeargle

abilities.name: Second Coat
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch a basic Energy card attached to your Active Pokémon with a different type of basic Energy card from your discard pile.

attacks.name: Horn Grab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Super Slice
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Ultra Space
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for an Ultra Beast card, reveal it, put it into their hand, and shuffle their deck.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Buzzwole-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Knuckle Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Absorption-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Alolan Persian-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Smug Face
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's TAG TEAM Pokémon and Ultra Beasts, and by your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.

attacks.name: Stalking Claws-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Thievul

attacks.name: Thief
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: M Altaria-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mist Purge
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

name: Shaymin LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Shaymin. Shaymin LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Revenge Seed
abilities.text: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, each of Shaymin's attacks does 60 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Flare
attacks.text: You may move any number of Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Bill's Analysis
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 Trainer cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Buck's Training
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 2 cards. As long as Buck's Training is next to your Active Pokémon, each of your Active Pokémon's attacks does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Dialga-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chrono Wind

attacks.text: Discard 2 Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Lucario & Melmetal-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Metal Wall-GX
attacks.text: For the rest of this game, your Metal Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rough Seas
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may heal 30 damage from each of his or her Water Pokémon and Lightning Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Plumeria
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack

name: Manaphy-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Apricorn Maker
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word "Ball" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Scizor VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hard Scissors
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Field Blower

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Charizard-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

name: Fire Memory

The Silvally-GX this card is attached to is a Fire Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Continuous Bite
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If you get 1 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If you get 2 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. If you get 3 heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 60 more damage.

name: Reuniclus

abilities.name: Damage Swap
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psywave

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

name: Forretress

attacks.name: Thorny Eruption
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lost Blast
attacks.text: Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters piece of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.

name: Electivire LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Electivire. Electivire LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.text: As long as Electivire is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pulse Barrier

name: Eviolite
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Audino

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number heads.

flavorText: Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Shuffle Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch all damage counters on Unown with those on the Defending Pokémon. (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

name: Scizor

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Bind Wound

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

name: Blastoise-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. The, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Trainer card from your discard pile, show it to your opponent, and then shuffle it into your deck.

attacks.name: Dark Clamp

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.name: Steely Spirit
abilities.text: Your Metal Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

abilities.name: Shuffle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Unown and switch it with Unown. (Any cards attached to Unown, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Unown on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
