rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damage by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Black Market ◇
rules: When a Darkness Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) that has any Darkness Energy attached to it is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card.
Whenever any player plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Stadium card.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Po Town
rules: Whenever any player plays a Pokémon from their hand to evolve 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Cynthia's Feelings
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

attacks.name: Nasty Goo
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola's waste-disposal site. They're all hard workers who eat a lot of trash.

name: Altaria-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

This card can't retreat.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Alolan Ninetales

attacks.name: Spiral Drain

flavorText: Possessing a calm demeanor, this Pokémon was revered as a deity incarnate before it was identified as a regional variant of Ninetales.

name: Rare Candy

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Shrine of Memories
rules: Each player's evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (That player still needs the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Energy Pickup
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Beast Energy ◇
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.

While this card is attached to an Ultra Beast, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. The attacks of the Ultra Beast this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

name: Rotom Dex Poké Finder Mode

name: Zygarde

name: Magmortar LV.X

Put 3 damage counters instead of 2 on that Pokémon between turns. This power can't be used if Magmortar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Magmortar. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) During your next turn, Magmortar can't use Flame Bluster.

name: Judge

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Archie's Ace in the Hole
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Put a Water Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Diggersby

attacks.text: Discard up to 6 cards from the top of your deck. If you do, this attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card other than a Baby Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Hala

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Togetic

flavorText: It grows dispirited if it is not with kind people. It can float in midair without moving its wings.

name: Noctowl

attacks.name: Carry Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. They shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck. Then, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

name: Leafeon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Grand Bloom-GX

name: Pokémon Communication
rules: Reveal a Pokémon from your hand and put it into your deck. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Feather Arrow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hollow Hunt-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rayquaza C LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Rayquaza C. Rayquaza C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Dragon Spirit
abilities.text: If Rayquaza C is your Active Pokémon and is damaged but not Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, you may search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza C.

attacks.name: Final Blowup
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to Rayquaza C. Ignore this effect if you have no cards in your hand.

name: Mawile-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Captivating Wink
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand and put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.

attacks.name: Wily Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Big Eater-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard all Supporter cards you find there. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Azelf

attacks.name: Shining Eyes
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

name: Whimsicott-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fluffy Cotton
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Energy Blow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Toy Box-GX
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 5 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Dive Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Water Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Bewear

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Big Throw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards.

flavorText: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

name: Xerneas ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Path of Life
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to become your Active Pokémon, you may move any number of Energy from your other Pokémon to it.

attacks.name: Bright Horns
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Bright Horns during your next turn.

flavorText: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

name: Professor's Letter
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Unidentified Fossil
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Colorless Basic Pokémon. At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Raikou

attacks.name: Lost Voltage
attacks.text: If you have any Lightning Energy cards in the Lost Zone, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

name: Rocky Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damage by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Incineroar-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hustling Strike
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Fire Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tiger Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Burning Slam-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Epic Splash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

name: Celesteela-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rocket Fall
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Blaster-GX
attacks.text: Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Skwovet
flavorText: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

name: Noivern-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Distort
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Sonic Volume
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Special Energy cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Boomburst-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Staraptor FB LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Staraptor FB. Staraptor FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Fast Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Staraptor FB is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Defog
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, this attack's base damage is 70 instead of 40.

name: Forest Guardian
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Shuffle your deck. Then, look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose 1 of those cards and put it into your hand. Shuffle the rest into your deck afterward.

name: Floette

abilities.name: Flower Picking
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

flavorText: It raises flowers and uses them as weapons. The more gorgeous the blossom, the more power it contains.

name: Trumbeak

abilities.name: Charging Trumpet
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: From its mouth, it fires the seeds of berries it has eaten. The scattered seeds give rise to new plants.

name: Master Ball
rules: Look at 7 cards from the top of your deck. You may choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from those cards, show it your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the rest into your deck.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Discerning Spear
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

name: Cutiefly

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Myriads of Cutiefly flutter above the heads of people who have auras resembling those of flowers.

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Spritzee

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

name: Team Skull Grunt
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard 2 Energy cards from it.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Oranguru

abilities.name: Primate Wisdom
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.

flavorText: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

name: Zygarde-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cell Connector
attacks.text: Attach 2 Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Verdict-GX
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Reserved Ticket
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Giratina

abilities.name: Distortion Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 1 damage counter on 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Impact
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

name: Viridian Forest
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard a card from their hand. If they do, that player searches their deck for a basic Energy card, reveals it, and puts it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Turtonator-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nitro Tank-GX
attacks.text: Attach 5 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Magnetic Pull
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spin Tackle
attacks.text: Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.

name: Dusk Mane Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Meteor Tempest
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sun's Eclipse-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Acerola
rules: Put 1 of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ribombee

abilities.name: Mysterious Buzz
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Fairy Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Some of Ribombee's pollen puffs are highly nutritious. They are sometimes sold as supplements.

name: Florges

abilities.name: Flower Picking
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It controls the flowers it grows. The petal blizzards that Florges triggers are overwhelming in their beauty and power.

name: Greedent

abilities.name: Greedy Tail
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.

name: Toucannon

attacks.name: Energy Cutoff
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loop Cannon
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Known for forming harmonious couples, this Pokémon is brought to wedding ceremonies as a good luck charm.

name: Articuno

attacks.name: Diamond Dust
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to freeze the water in the air during winter, causing snow.

name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Dust Gathering
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: To protect itself, it shoots cotton from its body. When it gets wet in the rain, its cotton grows moist and heavy, and it can't move as well.

name: Lurantis-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flower Supply
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Solar Blade
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chloroscythe-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Drawup Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

name: Grass Cube 01
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Grass Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Grass Cube 01.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

name: Yveltal-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Absorb Vitality
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sonic Evil
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Doom Count-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Yanmega

abilities.name: Sonic Vision
abilities.text: If you have exactly 4 cards in your hand, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon's attacks.

attacks.name: Assault Boom
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

name: Multi Technical Machine 01
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Multi Technical Machine 01.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Drampa

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

name: Aromatisse

attacks.name: Pungent Aroma
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, your opponent reveals their hand. For each heads, choose a card you find there. Your opponent shuffles those cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Miraculous Cologne
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

flavorText: It devises various scents, pleasant and unpleasant, and emits scents that its enemies dislike in order to gain an edge in battle.

name: Weakness Policy
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Skyla
rules: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Tapu Lele

abilities.name: Charmed Charm
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Pokémon Tool card that has "Fairy Charm" in its name from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may leave your opponent's Active Pokémon Confused.

flavorText: As it flutters about, it scatters its strangely glowing scales. Touching them is said to restore good health on the spot.

name: Water Memory
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Silvally-GX this card is attached to is a Water Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Primarina-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Beat
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Roaring Seas
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grand Echo-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Dialga-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overclock
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Timeless-GX
attacks.text: Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lapras-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Blizzard Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Ice Beam-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Guzma
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pokémon Nurse
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon. Then discard all Energy cards attached to it, if any.

name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Power Heater
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to each of those Pokémon.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Allister
rules: Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, discard up to 3 cards from your hand. (You must discard at least 1 card.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Wondrous Labyrinth ◇
rules: The attacks of non-Fairy Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) cost Colorless more.
Whenever any player plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Stadium card.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

name: Moltres

attacks.name: Hyper Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a Fire Energy attached to Moltres. If tails, discard all Energy attached to Moltres.

flavorText: The flames on this legendary Pokémon's wings burn so brightly that they can make night seem like day.

name: Rattata
flavorText: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

name: Dialga-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overclock
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Timeless-GX
attacks.text: Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Alolan Ninetales-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blizzard Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ice Path-GX
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rookidee
flavorText: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

name: Delcatty

attacks.name: Captivating Tail
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

name: Dubwool V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Soft Wool
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Super Rod
rules: Shuffle 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Whimsicott

abilities.name: Prowl
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This nuisance sneaks into people's homes, where it hides important things and scatters cotton all over the place.

name: Rowlet

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

name: Healing Berry
rules: Attach Healing Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool card attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
At the end of any turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has 20 HP or less, remove 3 damage counters from that Pokémon and discard this card.

name: Tate & Liza
rules:
Choose 1:
• Shuffle your hand into your deck then draw 5 cards.
• Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Morelull

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: As it drowses the day away, it nourishes itself by sucking from tree roots. It wakens at the fall of night, wandering off in search of a new tree.

name: Swirlix

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

name: Slurpuff

attacks.name: Olfactory Enchantment
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Sweet Panic
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Confused, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

name: Xerneas-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sanctuary-GX
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Bea
rules: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched Fighting Pokémon in any way you like.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Celebi & Venusaur-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Pollen Hazard
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Evergreen-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Grass Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), shuffle all cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Espeon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psychic

attacks.name: Continuous Blaze Ball
attacks.text: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When expelling a blast of superhot fire, the red flame at the tip of its tail burns more intensely.

name: Ampharos-GX

name: M Houndoom-EX

attacks.text: You may discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Dan
rules: Draw 2 cards. You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors until someone wins. If you win, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Ancient Ruins
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Once during each player's turn (before he or she attacks), if he or she has not played a Supporter card, that player may reveal his or her hand to his or her opponent. If that player reveals his or her hand and there is no Supporter card there, that player draws a card.

name: Naganadel & Guzzlord-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chaotic Order-GX
attacks.text: Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Psychic Energy and 1 extra Darkness Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), take 2 Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Golisopod-GX

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

name: Energy Recycler

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Dragonair

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

name: Staraptor

attacks.name: Brave Bird

Whenever an attack from the Pokémon that Strength Charm is attached to does damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage. At the end of your turn in which this happens, discard Strength Charm.

flavorText: The question of why only Eevee has such unstable genes has still not been solved.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Photon Wave
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to does damage with an attack (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage. At the end of every turn, put 1 damage counter on the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to, unless it's Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Lure Ball
rules: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, choose an Evolution card from your discard pile, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Pheromosa-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Cruel Spike
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Beauty-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Undersea Ruins
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Once during each player's turn (before attacking), that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player chooses 1 of his or her Evolved Pokémon in play and discards the top Evolution card from that Pokémon, devolving it.

name: Alolan Muk-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chemical Breath
attacks.text: This attack does 70 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Hazard-GX
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pikachu & Zekrom-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Blitz
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Tag Bolt-GX
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 170 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Porygon2

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: AI has been installed in it. It learns various things all on its own, but it even remembers things it doesn't need to know.

name: Rocky Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Haxorus

abilities.name: Grind Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, attach up to 3 in any combination of Fire and Metal Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Axe
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage times the amount of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Rookidee

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

name: Tool Scrapper
rules: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Max Potion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Dana
rules: You can play this card only if your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon.
Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
